description: The CAPA record (ISO 10.2 corrective action) the buyer opens with a supplier, usually via a SCAR (Supplier Corrective Action Request), when an NCR cause is systemic. The supplier submits root cause (5-why), corrective and preventive action and a target date; Quality reviews effectiveness; the result feeds the supplier re-evaluation (8.4.1) and may suspend the supplier. It is the 10.2 node of the closed ISO loop 8.6 to 8.7 to 10.2 to 8.4.1, and CAPA responsiveness feeds the supplier scorecard responsiveness dimension.

fields.name: identifier
fields.label: CAPA / SCAR No.
fields.validation: system pattern; unique; immutable once opened
fields.purpose: Stable key for the corrective-action case so the supplier response, the effectiveness review and the re-evaluation feed all trace to one CAPA and back to its NCR; the ISO 10.2 record handle.

fields.name: ncrId
fields.label: Source NCR
fields.validation: resolves to the NCR whose systemic cause triggered this CAPA; not null
fields.purpose: Anchors the corrective action to the non-conformance that triggered it, so closing the CAPA with verified effectiveness lets the source NCR close (axiom A11); one NCR can open a CAPA when the cause is systemic.

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.purpose: Names the supplier the SCAR is issued to; CAPA responsiveness and effectiveness feed this supplier's re-evaluation (8.4.1) and the consecutive-below counter that gates suspension (axiom A11).

fields.name: rootCause
fields.label: Root Cause
fields.purpose: The supplier's documented root-cause analysis (5-why) of why the non-conformance occurred; the basis ISO 10.2 requires before a corrective action can be judged adequate.

fields.name: action
fields.label: Corrective Action
fields.validation: non-empty; max 2000 chars; the immediate correction taken
fields.purpose: The immediate correction the supplier applies to fix the identified cause; the corrective half of the ISO 10.2 action whose effectiveness Quality then reviews.

fields.name: preventiveAction
fields.label: Preventive Action
fields.validation: non-empty; max 2000 chars; systemic change to prevent recurrence
fields.purpose: The systemic change the supplier makes so the non-conformance does not recur; the preventive half of ISO 10.2 and what the effectiveness review verifies actually holds.

fields.name: targetDate
fields.label: Target Date
fields.validation: valid date; the supplier response SLA
fields.purpose: The response-SLA date by which the supplier must complete the action; CAPA responsiveness against this date feeds the supplier scorecard responsiveness dimension.

fields.name: evidence
fields.label: Evidence
fields.validation: document / image files; supplier evidence of the action taken
fields.purpose: The supplier-supplied proof (revised procedures, photos, test data) that the corrective and preventive action were implemented; what Quality inspects to judge effectiveness.

fields.name: effectivenessReview
fields.label: Effectiveness Review
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when Quality reviews the action; mandatory before the CAPA (and its NCR) can close
fields.validation: effective closes the CAPA and NCR and feeds re-evaluation; not-effective re-opens the SCAR (loop)
fields.purpose: Quality's verdict on whether recurrence is actually prevented: effective closes the CAPA and source NCR and feeds the re-evaluation; not-effective re-opens the SCAR and feeds the consecutive-below counter toward suspension (axiom A11).

fields.name: isSCAR
fields.label: Is SCAR
fields.validation: true when issued to the supplier as a formal Supplier Corrective Action Request; false for an internal-only corrective action
fields.purpose: Marks whether the corrective action is a formal SCAR issued to the supplier (replace / credit / corrective action with a response SLA) versus an internal-only action; a SCAR is what counts toward the supplier responsiveness score.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: open on issue; action-proposed when the supplier submits; under-review during effectiveness review; closed when effective; re-opened when not-effective
fields.purpose: The CAPA lifecycle position; an unresolved or not-effective active CAPA is a suspend trigger and keeps the source NCR open, which keeps the requisition out of COMPLETED (axioms A3, A11).

fields.name: feedsReEvaluationAt
fields.label: Re-evaluation Fed At
fields.validation: set when the closed CAPA result is written to the supplier re-evaluation period (8.4.1)
fields.purpose: Timestamps when the closed CAPA was fed to the supplier re-evaluation (8.4.1), recomputing the period scorecard and the consecutive-below counter; the link from the 10.2 node to the 8.4.1 node of the loop (axiom A11).

invariants: A3 an unresolved/not-effective CAPA blocks the requisition reaching COMPLETED
A10 SUSPENDED blocks new POs without offboarding
A11 CAPA closes the loop into 8.4.1 re-evaluation and may suspend the supplier; not-effective re-opens the SCAR
A13 audit + SSE on every CAPA change
an NCR with an open CAPA closes only when effectiveness is verified (ISO 10.2)
suspend is event-driven and split across Quality / Tax-Compliance / Admin by permission
